label: MusicBrainz Recording Search
summary: MusicBrainz recording search is the broadest provider-neutral SongMetadata fallback because it can search by artist/title and still return recording length.
That also makes it the most ambiguity-prone candidate in this repo, so its value is resilience rather than first-choice authority.
It deserves a real atlas row because the open API and JS tooling are strong, but it should sit behind provider-affine enrichers and behind MusicBrainz ISRC lookup.

signals: MusicBrainz recording search supports artist/artistname/recording fields.
Recording length is a first-class property in MusicBrainz.
This is useful for fallback resilience, but clearly weaker than identifier-driven lookups.

notes: The JS client is MIT licensed and the underlying MusicBrainz service is open, though search etiquette and overmatching control still matter.

entries.subject: Borewit/musicbrainz-api
notes: Maintained JavaScript client and direct fit for host-owned or browser-owned MusicBrainz integration.

entries.subject: MusicBrainz Search API
notes: The service is public, but the adapter still needs search etiquette, User-Agent discipline, and bounded result selection.
The API is open and the tooling is good.
The real burden is fallback correctness and overmatching avoidance, not runtime implementation complexity.

methods.notes: Open HTTP and no auth make this feasible locally, but the adapter still needs strict search-result discipline.
There is no engine to port.
A native bridge adds no obvious value to an open search API.
Possible, but weaker than plain JS or a shared host.
Too brittle for a reusable search fallback lane.
Fine, but not the strongest evidence path.
Strong place to centralize search heuristics, scoring, and cache policy.
Adds indirection to an already search-policy-heavy candidate.
Already available through JS tooling and direct HTTP.
Possible when Axolync wants centralized search heuristics, but still likely heavier than needed.
Too heavy for a broad fallback search path.
No platform SDK story exists here.

notes: Keep this row visible because it is a real resilience option, but do not confuse it with a first-choice canonical-duration source.
It should run after provider-affine enrichers and after MusicBrainz ISRC lookup.

openQuestions: How strict should artist/title fallback matching be before SongMetadata refuses to return duration?
Should this path be disabled entirely in strict profiles that only allow provider-affine or identifier-based metadata enrichers?
